forward_condition.condition: The caller has a damaged, wrong, missing, or return issue and enough detail to research it
The case has enough evidence to decide whether email is viable or a manual path is required
A support email exists and email is the strongest path
Email is unavailable or the merchant requires a form, account flow, or phone support
The draft is created or sent and the caller understands the next step
The caller understands the exact manual path and fallback plan
The caller has no more questions and is satisfied

greeting.additional_prompt: Identify whether the caller has a real damaged, wrong, missing, or return issue. Gather the merchant, issue type, and whether proof or order evidence exists before moving on.
greeting.label: Identify Refund Problem

qualification.additional_prompt: Explain whether the caller likely qualifies, what visible evidence matters, and what the strongest next step is. Keep the answer grounded and practical, not speculative.
qualification.label: Evidence & Qualification

channel.additional_prompt: Choose the best medium for action. Prefer support email when it exists and is the strongest path. Use manual fallback when the merchant requires a support form, account flow, or phone support.
channel.label: Choose Resolution Channel

email_resolution.additional_prompt: Guide the user through draft generation, draft review, and send. Make the email-first path feel like the fastest, strongest move, but do not send before approval.
email_resolution.label: Draft & Send Email

manual_resolution.additional_prompt: Explain the manual next step when email is not the right medium. Be explicit about whether the user should use a support form, account flow, or phone path and what to do next.
manual_resolution.label: Manual Support Path

resolve.additional_prompt: Summarize the verdict, the evidence used, what was done, what happens next, and the fallback plan. End with one concrete next step and no ambiguity.
resolve.label: Resolve & Next Steps
